issues.description: In the dashboard, the distribution charts lack interactivity, which prevents users from viewing the full names of all distribution categories. Several labels are truncated and cannot be expanded or accessed through interaction (e.g., hover or click), limiting the ability to fully interpret the distribution details. new filter button of Región (division_02), Finca (division_01)
The selector displays Guatemala; however, when it is selected, no products, analyses, or data are shown on the dashboard.
Update product names: replace “sg” with “Smart Growth (SG)” and “maleza_temporalidad” with “Malezas” to ensure consistent and user-friendly labeling in the application.
Images are not being published on time; they are uploaded after the satellite pass.
The raster clip is not being applied at the Pantaleon level and is missing in some parcels, resulting in imagery being displayed outside the expected boundaries.
A configuration or detection mechanism must be implemented for users who rely on action buttons, as the current interaction interferes with navigation and prevents them from proceeding.

The scale-dependent rendering effect that visually enlarges polygons should be removed. At distant zoom levels, entire fields appear highlighted in red and change as the user zooms in, which can cause confusion when viewing the overall map.

In the Comparison section, the color contrast does not provide adequate visibility for outdoor field conditions. Exposure to sunlight reduces text legibility, which can hinder effective use of the platform during field activities.

For field GT01-0010157-0104, the Comparison view shows partial data coverage for the SG layer, while NDVI Potential displays complete coverage for the same field, despite the fact that the corresponding template contains valid field information (e.g., crop start date, variety).
Additionally, only the SG legend is displayed, while the NDVI Potential legend is missing, which limits proper interpretation of the comparative results.

A minimum and maximum display scale should be defined for the model/product layers to prevent information from disappearing when zooming in or out. Currently, as the user approaches the field level, the model or product information is no longer visible, which affects data continuity and interpretation.

In both the Explore section (map-based product views) and the Dashboard, the active campaign context is not clearly indicated. It is not specified whether the user is interacting with all campaigns, 2026, or 2027, which can lead to ambiguity when interpreting maps, products, and analytical results.

When navigating within the product views (e.g., NDVI, Weeds, NDVI Potential, NDWI, NDWI Potential), changing the selected date causes the platform to switch unexpectedly to the SG product. For example, when viewing data for 28/01/2026 and selecting 02/02/2026, the active product changes automatically to SG instead of remaining on the originally selected product
The filter mask shows movement when zooming out or zooming in.

In the inspections section, insert the point at the user's current position. Additionally, the inspection submission process needs to be corrected, as it currently does not allow submission; it only allows data entry, but the information is neither saved nor sent.
Add to the filters one by farm name, another by parcel code and ownership (under administration and producers)- filters division_01 as Finca, division_02 as Region
Not all polygon boundaries are visible on the map (all boundaries are visible within the web portal).
The plot description displays information from the master plot (variety, area, start date, productivity, average of the visualized index)
An icon is desired within the legend that describes the product viewed and expands on the description of each color.

Add an interactive information icon (i or ?) exclusively to the map legend widget within the mobile app. Tapping it will open a pop-up window or text box with a detailed explanation of the displayed symbols.

Expected behavior:
The icon should be visible next to the legend title or in a corner of the legend.
Tapping it will display a clear description. For example, on a potential map, it would explain the meaning of "High," "Medium," and "Low" in terms of crop development.
The text should be concise and easy to read on mobile device screens.

A list should be implemented that allows the selection of multiple items using checkboxes. When one or more options are selected, the associated data fields (such as "Dose" and "Unit") should be marked as required. To ensure data integrity, the system should validate that these fields are complete, keeping the action button disabled or displaying an error if an attempt is made to save without the required information. Additionally, the text of the "Submit Inspection" button should be changed to "Submit."

The SL App should include a measurement tool (“Ruler”) to allow users to measure distances directly within the map interface. Additionally, an “Add Polygon” functionality should be implemented to enable users to draw polygons within the application, similar to the drawing tools available in Google Earth. When creating a polygon, the system should require the identifier field (ABS_IDCOMP / Parcel ID) as a mandatory attribute. The created polygon should also be downloadable from the platform in SHP and KML formats to support further spatial analysis and integration with external GIS tools.

The Current Weather Conditions section should include additional climate variables such as relative humidity and wind speed to provide a more comprehensive view of environmental conditions. Additionally, when a parcel is selected, the applied filter should also be reflected in the weather data, ensuring that climate information corresponds to the selected parcel context.

The platform should include the RGB image alongside the analytical products and display the corresponding cloud coverage percentage (%) for each image, enabling better visual validation of conditions and data quality during analysis.

In the mobile application, the location point for inspections should not be editable or movable by the user. The system should only allow capturing the current device location to ensure accuracy and reliability of georeferenced inspection data.
Distribution charts for products are currently displayed at the operational zone level; however, it is required to update the visualization to the region level instead.

In the Field Operations (Labores) section, the interface should be interactive by displaying polygons that are selectable on the map. Users should be able to select one or multiple polygons and input or update data for all selected features simultaneously, enabling efficient bulk data entry and management.

In the mobile application, within the Inspection section, the interface does not properly adjust to the screen size. As a result, action buttons such as “Apply Changes” are partially hidden or overlapped, preventing users from interacting with them and completing the process.

The platform should incorporate ejido-level information for Amajac, EMSA, IPSA, into the database and make it available as a filter, allowing users—particularly supervisors—to filter and analyze data at the ejido level for improved segmentation and operational management.

The soil moisture variable used in the Growth Index (SG) should be corrected by aligning it with the NDWI-derived moisture values, ensuring consistency between indices and improving the accuracy of the growth analysis.
In IPSA, a resolution issue has been observed in the generation of map tiles, where the rendered imagery appears pixelated and inconsistent with the expected spatial resolution

In Amajac, IPSA, EMSA, not all parcel boundaries are fully visible on the map, as only approximately 5,000 parcels are displayed, while the total number of parcels exceeds this limit. This restriction limits proper spatial interpretation and field-level analysis.

The platform should include a new irrigation priority product focused on crop management. This product should integrate soil moisture percentage (%) and identify water stress conditions to support irrigation decision-making and optimize field management practices.

Within the Inspection module, inspections can be successfully submitted when no image is attached. However, when an image is added, the inspection is not submitted, preventing the record from being saved.

The platform should implement PSH access monitoring, enabling user management by mill (Pantaleon, Monte Rosa, Amajac, IPSA, EMSA) and providing visibility into user sessions, including login activity by user and date, to support tracking, auditing, and usage analysis
The dashboard should display a harvest performance summary at the time of data update, including key indicators such as productivity, number of fields, and hectares harvested (ha).

For field GT01-0010123-0207, in the Weed Detection model, an aggregated red area is displayed at certain zoom levels. When zooming in further, this aggregation disappears and only the underlying pixel-level detections remain visible. This scale-dependent rendering overrepresents the affected area and may lead to misinterpretation of weed distribution.
R² value in the Performance section does not match the R² shown on the model card in Home. Align calculations/source.
“Analyzed Samples” KPI is inconsistent (should be ~20k but shows ~4k). Verify counting logic and source.
Rename selected features to be more user-friendly (e.g., image bands like B2/B3/etc. should display meaningful names).
Reduce polygon border thickness on the cluster map and hide polygon outlines when zoomed out (match behavior from other maps).
Reorder cluster priority for harvest decisions based on moisture value (clusters should be ordered by humidity, not current ordering).
For cluster analysis, add a chart that displays current moisture, previous moisture, and cluster color (cluster-coded series/legend) to support interpretation.
In “Area by Region”, the bar chart should be segmented (stacked or grouped) by cluster.
In “Area Under/Over Curve”, region bars must be colored by curve status (under/over/within curve categories).
In “Criticality by Stage”, region bars must be colored by curve/status category (consistent status coloring across regions).
Apply consistent parcel polygon border behavior across all maps in Results Analysis (reduce borders and hide outlines when zoomed out).
For all region bar charts across subsections, display category values on the bars (data labels per region/category).

The satellite-based moisture monitor is not updated for Monte Rosa (05/01/2026) and Pantaleon (03/01/2026). Additionally, the current moisture TIFF cannot be downloaded, which prevents validation of related products such as clustering, under/over the curve analysis, growth stage criticality, and irrigation priority.

In Monte Rosa, when downloading the moisture TIFF, in the platform, the output incorrectly references GT instead of NI. This indicates an issue with the country or context identification in the export process, which may lead to confusion and incorrect data association.
Add the option in the filters to search by farm (division_01) and id_parcela.

In Monte Rosa, when downloading the moisture TIFF in  the API, the output incorrectly references GT instead of NI. For example: "https://s..com/cogs-bucket/cogs/GT_Grupo_Pantaleon_Humedad_2026-03-26_cog.tif"

Implement a segmentation of models for humidity, restricting calibration to records older than 7 months, for the application of ripening agent, in order to ensure the consistency and representativeness of the data used in the model adjustment.
Incorporate a filter by plot, farm and region within the humidity model, in order to allow the segmentation and specific analysis of the data at the production unit level.

Establish the relationship between the NDVI+ and Hydro+ indices and the plots corresponding to the research trials, conditioning its implementation on the validation and certainty of the humidity model.

Filters for the moisture model
The same filters currently available in the remote sensing module should also be applied to the moisture model, in order to maintain functional consistency and improve usability for end users.

In the GEOIA Models section, metrics are not recalculated or refreshed when switching between different mills or organizations.
All KPIs and metrics should update dynamically based on the selected organizational context.
Failing to do so can lead to incorrect interpretation of model performance and analytics.

The platform should provide historical predictive capabilities that identify which past harvest season (zafra) current conditions most closely resemble, based on correlations with key indices (e.g., NDVI, NDWI, SG), in order to support estimation and forecasting.

The platform should include a new product to detect anomalies in index behavior, identifying abrupt increases or decreases in values that may be associated with events such as weed presence or field operations (e.g., tillage(volteo)). This capability should analyze temporal variations in key indices (e.g., NDVI, NDWI, SG) to highlight significant deviations and support timely decision-making for Amajac, EMSA, IPSA
“Active Models” KPI must count only active models, excluding models in development.
“Average Precision” KPI must be calculated using only active models.
“Analyzed Lots” KPI is hardcoded. It should be the average number of lots in the most recent prediction across active models.
“Analyzed Area” KPI is hardcoded. It should be the average lot area in the most recent prediction across active models.
Add a “Last Updated” KPI/label showing the date of the latest training for active models.
Ensure “Training Zafras” are computed dynamically (not hardcoded) based on each model’s training data.
Add a Harvest Season (Zafra) filter across all sections, including map layers and analytics, so data is scoped consistently by zafra.
Add a toggle to show/hide parcel polygons on all maps, and enable a parcel information pop-up when clicking a polygon.

When clicking the PDF button located next to the “Grupo Pantaleon” label, the platform becomes unresponsive.
No PDF is generated, and all buttons and interactions stop working after the action is triggered.

When Google Chrome dark mode is enabled, text within the platform is rendered in a pale gray color, reducing readability. In standard (light) mode, the platform displays the text correctly with adequate contrast.
Right-click interactions within the platform override the default browser context menu, limiting the availability of standard options such as opening content in a new tab.
The platform currently does not support an in-platform split-screen or side-by-side view for comparing NDVI+, NDVI Potential, or other derived products within the same workspace.
When adding a TIFF download option for model outputs, support for RGB composites should also be considered.
Pantaleon’s images are not being published on time; they are uploaded four days after the satellite pass.

Within the platform, certain products and models display intermittent data availability across image dates. For example, in the NDVI product for Monte Rosa, no data is available for several fields between 31/12/2025 and 15/01/2026, data becomes available on 20/01/2026, and is again unavailable between 25/01/2026 and 30/01/2026.
This occurs despite the fact that the corresponding template contains valid field information (e.g., crop start date, variety). 
evaluate if it was due to active season

Pixel overlap between adjacent polygons has been observed. Although a buffer is applied to constrain the analysis area, this clipping is not consistently reflected across all areas. As a result, pixels from neighboring polygons are included in certain cases, leading to inaccuracies in the calculated values.

When applying filters within the platform, the map only zooms to the selected area but does not restrict the display to the selected elements. For example, when filtering Region 1 and a specific field in Amajac, the map zooms to the selected location, but other fields remain visible instead of showing only the filtered selection.

Across several monitors, the reported parcel areas do not match the monitored area values, and this discrepancy is observed consistently across multiple fields and total area calculations. For example, for field MX07-5644011-0001, the NDVI+ Monitor reports 5 ha as the monitored area, while the NDVI Range Distribution shows 4.5 ha, and both the NDVI vs Potential Distribution and Parcel Details show 14.3 ha. In the Growth Monitor (SG), the monitored area is 5 ha, but the Growth Index Distribution shows 15 ha, and Areas by Improvement Status per Stage shows 14.8 ha. In the NDWI Monitor, the monitored area is 8 ha, while the Hydro+ (NDWI) Range Distribution reports 14.8 ha, and the Weed Monitor reports 12.53 ha. Similar discrepancies are observed in other fields; for example, field MX07-5127038-0001 shows 9 ha in the NDVI+ Monitor, while the NDVI Range Distribution reports 8.7 ha, and the NDVI vs Potential Distribution shows 8.5 ha. In the Growth Monitor (SG), the monitored area is 9 ha, while Areas by Improvement Status per Stage shows 8.5 ha. In the NDWI Monitor, the monitored area is 9 ha, while the Hydro+ (NDWI) Range Distribution shows 8.5 ha, and the Weed Monitor reports 9.65 ha. This issue appears consistently across different monitors and fields, where the monitored area, distribution values, and total reported areas do not align, which may affect the reliability and interpretation of the analytical results.
No se esta visualizando en todas las parcelas la curva real con respecto al potencia (NDVI Potencial e Hidro Potencial)
En la opcion de descargar en formato de excel no esta funcionando correctamente, lo descarga corrompido y por tal razon toda la informacion queda en una sola columna

When downloading Detailed Data by Parcel for the SG, NDVI, NDWI, and Weed monitors, the exported CSV file only includes the records currently displayed on the first page of the table (e.g., 1–20 of 7,382 records) instead of exporting the complete dataset. Additionally, when opening the CSV file and converting the data from text to columns, some values appear misaligned or shifted into incorrect columns, which affects proper data interpretation and analysis.

In the NDVI+ and Hydro+ potential module, the dialog box with the potential curve for the selected plot in Mexico (EMSA, AMAJAC, IPSA) is not displayed. The selected plot is also not highlighted in red. The data loading or display logic should be reviewed.No visualiza la curva potencial en MX

It is necessary to change the write-type filters to list-type filters in the detailed data section by parcel, since the filters applied in the header do not affect or work correctly on this detailed view.

When exporting the map to PDF, the content appears misaligned and compressed at the top of the document, leaving a large blank space at the bottom. The map's centering and scale need to be adjusted so that it correctly fills the available area in the PDF layout. Normalize the categories by country (send the list of distinct values ​​first and we will send the normalization for the historical data), but ensure that the zafra feild is used in the join.

Extend the functionality of the information icon (such as the one shown for the "Total Monitored Area" KPI) to all key visual components of the platform. Hovering over this icon will display a tooltip with details about the element.

Affected components:
KPI: Add a clear description of the metric being displayed (e.g., "Total area of ​​parcels under active satellite monitoring").
Charts: Explain what the data, axes, or how to interpret the visualized information represents.
Maps: Provide information about the data layers, the symbology used, or the source of the geospatial information.
Table of contents: Clarify the meaning of specific columns or the nature of the listed data.

The current filename generated for TIFF image downloads is too long, making them difficult to manage and organize. A new naming convention needs to be implemented that is short, consistent, and contains only the essential information.

The new filename structure should be as follows:

[Site Code]_[Product]_[Date].tif

The current legend of the potential map (as seen in the reference image) displays numerical ranges (e.g., <0.6, 0.9–1.1) that are not self-explanatory. For clarity and to facilitate user interpretation, these labels need to be modified to explicitly indicate that they represent percentage values.

Currently, numerical values ​​in the platform's various modules (KPIs, tables, charts) are displayed with an inconsistent number of decimal places. This creates visual confusion and can hinder quick data comparison. A single rule for decimal display needs to be defined and applied.

Rule Specification:
All numerical values ​​on the platform representing metrics, averages, percentages, or measurements must be displayed with two (2) decimal places, taking into account the following considerations:

General Application: This rule applies to:
Values ​​in KPIs and summary cards.
Numbers within tables.
Data labels and axes in charts.
Information displayed in tooltips and pop-up windows (maps, charts, etc.).
Numerical data in exports (CSV, Excel), where applicable.

Zero Padding: Whole numbers or numbers with only one decimal place must be padded with zeros to maintain two decimal places. This ensures alignment and visual consistency in tables.

Exceptions: Values ​​that by their nature are always whole numbers (e.g., number of farms, number of lots, number of inspections) must be displayed without decimals.

A problem has been detected in the numeric display system, where the grouping of digits (thousands and decimal separators) does not follow the expected format according to the regional settings. This causes inconsistencies in the reading and interpretation of data, especially in indicators and graphs.
Configure the display filter to show only the selected records, hiding all those that do not meet the defined criteria.

Incorporate filters that allow segmenting information based on the application status of the ripening agent (applied/not applied) and the crop's condition. Additionally, evaluate implementing a dynamic selector as an improvement, allowing users to define the filtering attribute, whether based on tabular data or images, according to its applicability. Display the most frequently used selections first.

Define a classification of potential in four categorical ranges, establishing the following intervals: Critical (<55), Improvement (55–65), Good (65–80) and Excellent (>80), in order to standardize the interpretation of the values. ---> change the ranges in script for classification)
Persist the zoom level on all maps when making product changes in the remote sensing module, in order to maintain continuity in the visualization of the area of ​​interest.

Implement a "Sticky Header" or "Freeze Pane" behavior for the top filter section (Zafra, Finca, Region, etc.). The filter bar must remain anchored at the top of the viewport while scrolling through the page to ensure filtering criteria are always visible and accessible.

Supervisor filter and multi-farm selection
The Pantaleón team would like to have a supervisor-based filter, as well as a multi-selection option for up to 20 farms. This would allow each supervisor to view only the farms assigned to them. At the moment, this functionality is not available in the platform.

TIFF file download behavior
When a single farm or a specific plot is selected, the TIFF download currently exports the entire map instead of only the selected area. The export should respect the applied filter and download only the selected farm or plot.
The “Average Hydro+ Index” indicator is displayed as a percentage, but it should be shown as an index value (not %).
The “High Deficit (< -0.2)” indicator appears to be miscalculated. Review the calculation and display the correct hectares (ha).
The “Water Balance” indicator calculation needs review. It should be computed as Optimal + Excellent.
The “Clear Filters” button is not working.
Implement a strategy for cumulative/connected filters so that selections apply coherently across filters (dependent filtering).
The range distribution may be incorrect, or the legend color mapping is wrong. Verify bins and ensure legend colors match the map/chart.
Rename “Balance by Farm” to “Status by Farm”.
Re-evaluate the categories and recommendations in the final data table, and add area (ha) by index range 1–14.

When switching between companies (Pantaleon, Monte Rosa, Amajac, IPSA, EMSA), the Hydro model retains the date from the previously selected company and requires a manual adjustment to refresh the correct date.

In the Hydro model, within the Detailed Water Status Analysis section, numeric values are displayed with excessive decimal precision (more than 10 decimal places), which makes the information difficult to read. Limiting the display to two decimal places would improve clarity and readability without affecting analytical accuracy.
Raster download option (button).
Soil moisture is hardcoded. Connect it to Open-Meteo Weather Variable (Soil Moisture 9–27 cm).
Climate indicators are hardcoded.
Stress Zone KPI does not match the right tail of the Growth Index histogram (KPI shows 910 ha, histogram shows 752 ha). Values must match.
Analysis filters are not updating the map. Filter field names are not using aliases (shows Division 1, Division 2, etc.).
Calendar should only enable dates for which imagery is available.
Change the TechnoAnalytics label.
SG color palette labels do not match the color palette displayed on the map. Focus red color
Only process images on the “standard” calendar; ignore atypical/off-calendar dates.
“Improvement Status by Stage” graph shows lots already in Macollamiento stage, but they don’t appear in the top “Phenological Stages” widget.
PDF generation is taking too long.
PDF is still showing TechnoAnalytics branding.

Analysis filters currently require users to manually scroll through long lists of values.
Add a search input for each filter category (e.g., Farm) to allow users to type and quickly find values.
Additionally, include the Parcel identifier (ABS_IDCOMP / Terreno) as a selectable filter.

In the Areas with Deficient Index by Cycle chart (hectares with deficient index per crop cycle) for SG, the cycle values are displayed inconsistently. Numeric values appear duplicated as both integers and decimals (e.g., 6 and 6.0, 7 and 7.0). Additionally, the cycle categories are not ordered numerically; instead, they follow a lexicographical order (e.g., 1, 10, 11, 12, 13, 2), which affects correct interpretation of the data distribution.
Add interactivity or a filter to the map to visualize the area by state type (critical, low, medium, good, and optimal)

In the Growth Monitor (SG), selecting a specific Farm does not correctly apply the expected filters to the Growth Index Distribution, Areas with Deficient Index by Cycle, Areas with Deficient Index by Phenological Stage, and Areas by Improvement Status per Stage sections. Instead of updating to display data corresponding to the selected farm, these components return no data, indicating that the filter context is not being properly applied across the dashboard.
Add a tooltip on hover that displays the indicator’s detailed explanation when the user places the cursor over it, to help with interpretation.
Update the “Minimum NDVI” indicator to use the 30th percentile (P30) instead of the current calculation.

In the NDVI Monitor, selecting a specific Farm (Finca) does not properly filter the NDVI Detailed Data by Parcel section. Instead of displaying only the parcels associated with the selected farm, all parcels remain visible, indicating that the filter is not being applied correctly to the detailed data view.

In the NDVI Detailed Data by Parcel section, the Region and Quadrant filters are not applied correctly, as the data does not update according to the selected values. Additionally, there is inconsistency in the naming conventions across countries, which may lead to confusion and affect filtering and data interpretation. For example, in Amajac, IPSA, EMSA, values such as REGION_01, REGION1, REGION2, REGION_02 are displayed; in Pantaleon, REGION OESTE, REGION CENTRAL, REGION1, REGION_01; and in Monte Rosa, ZONA 1, PR, REGION7, REGION_07, among others.

Parcel boundaries are difficult to distinguish due to the similarity in color tones between the parcel outlines and the TIFF layer. This lack of visual contrast causes the boundaries to blend with the underlying imagery, making it challenging to clearly identify parcel limits during analysis.
The Plot Map - Smart Growth Index of PANTALEON, MONTE ROSA, IPSA, EMSA and AMAJAC are not being affected by the header filter in time, it shows a delay in displaying the result.

Unify the display structure of the SG module maps to ensure consistent functionality and performance. The unified map should display and function similarly to the NDVI and Potential maps. Currently, the Smart Growth Index map zooms out when the date changes, while the SG map with variability remains static on the selected plot (this modification has been requested for all maps).
Update the symbology used in the Plot Map - Smart Growth Index to improve the visual interpretation of the data. Change it to the calculated field of growth index levels or improvement status.
The downloaded PDF generated from the map is not georeferenced
The “Control Program” indicator is currently hardcoded and needs to be connected to real data.
Set the weed critical threshold to 10% for Pantaleon (update configuration/business rule accordingly).
The data table should display only records from the latest available image, or from the image currently selected on the map.

In the Weed Detection monitor, the main indicators are displayed at the bottom of the map, unlike the NDVI+, Hydro+, and Growth monitors, where indicators are consistently shown at the top.
The indicator layout should be standardized across all monitors to ensure a consistent user experience.

In the weed model visualization, detections are observed in areas located outside the polygon boundary, including non-cultivated surfaces such as access areas and forested zones. Additionally, the detected patterns appear highly regular, which is not consistent with real-world weed distribution and may indicate inaccuracies in the spatial masking or classification process.

Currently, the platform exhibits inconsistent behavior when filtering by terrain.
With weed data (Image 1): When selecting a terrain with a recorded weed percentage, all KPIs, charts, and tables are displayed correctly.
Without weed data (Image 2): When selecting a terrain with a weed percentage of 0 or where data is missing, the visual components of the dashboard (KPIs, charts, and tables) disappear completely. This gives the false impression that the page has crashed or that there is no data, when in reality the basic terrain information is available.

Requirement and expected behavior:
To ensure consistency and clarity of information, the platform should behave as follows, regardless of the weed percentage value:

Display weed metrics as zero:
Any KPI, chart, or table that directly measures the "% of weeds" should not disappear. Instead, it should explicitly display the value as 0%. This confirms to the user that the data has been processed and that the result is zero.

Keep general KPIs visible:
KPIs and components that depend on general field information, such as area, lot name, date, etc., should always remain visible. This data is independent of weed percentage and should not be hidden by any filter of this type.
Incorporate an indicator that quantifies the total hectares analyzed in the weed monitoring, in order to measure the coverage of the analysis performed.

Segment weed detection into two distinct analytical models, establishing a first model for the range of 0 to 100 days and a second model for values ​​greater than 200 days, in order to optimize the accuracy and processing of data according to its magnitude.
Incorporate the label “In development” in the header of the weed detection module, in order to indicate its current status within the implementation process.
Incorporate an indicator that calculates the accuracy percentage of the weed detection model, in order to evaluate its level of precision with respect to the expected results.
Modify the legend reference in the weed detection module, replacing the image count with a measurement based on days (5, 10, 15, 20 and 25 days) in order to standardize the analysis over time.
Apply a clipping operation using a 12-meter buffer generated from the original polygon, in order to delimit the analysis area according to this spatial expansion.
Integrate the weed detection module within the GEOIA Models section, in order to centralize its management and align it with the weed detection development structure.

Within the weed detection module in the GEOIA Models section, components for analyzing model performance and developing data profiles should be incorporated in order to evaluate its performance and characterize the quality and behavior of the information used.

Weed color visualization
Weed level 1 should be represented with a stronger, more contrasting color, as it currently tends to blend in and is not clearly visible on the platform. This limitation could become even more critical in field conditions, especially in the App, where sunlight may further reduce screen visibility.

Two completely independent and dedicated analytics dashboards must be created in a dedicated section, such as NDVI+ and Hydro+. Each dashboard will have subsections for Inspections and Tasks. Each dashboard must have its own relevant KPIs, charts, and tables.

Inspections Dashboard:
Access: This dashboard will be accessed from the "Inspections" section.
Content: It should only display metrics and visualizations related to the data collected during inspections.

Tasks Dashboard:
Access: This dashboard will be accessed from the "Tasks" section.

Content: It should only display metrics and visualizations related to the execution and progress of agricultural tasks.

Update the terminology used in the indicator, replacing the label "zone without sugarcane" with a more appropriate name according to the defined criteria. (review the value for this kpi, "Sin vegetacion activa detectada"

In Guatemala, the app takes more than one minute to load the layers on startup, and this occurs every time the app is opened. This could indicate a bug or another issue affecting the initial loading process. Please investigate what may be causing the slow initial load.

When performing an inspection, as shown in the video, the app tends to slow down. In addition, it does not allow you to change the status because the button is hidden underneath the phone’s bottom bar, as shown in the picture
Calculate and model the optimal curve within the SGI, with the objective of establishing a reference behavior that allows comparing and evaluating the performance of the analyzed data.

Integrate a dynamic map legend within the full-screen view. The legend must display the color-coded categories and their corresponding values (e.g., NDVI ranges, moisture levels) to ensure data interpretability remains available when the map is expanded.
Within the map visualization, the TIFF imagery presents patchy spatial coverage within the same parcel, where areas of valid data are interspersed with gaps lacking coverage.
